observation: Task 569 completed all stages successfully (taskify through ship) for the truncate utility, but the verify stage reported TypeScript typecheck errors in .next/types/validator.ts related to PagesPageConfig constraints and missing 'default' properties in test files. These errors appear unrelated to the truncate utility itself (which was correctly identified as already existing in src/utils/truncate.ts). The pipeline proceeded to ship despite verify failures, suggesting verify stage errors may not be properly gating progression.
patternMatch: This is the first recorded run, but the pattern of verify stage errors not blocking ship is consistent with the previous run #544 (isEven utility) where verify also showed failures yet the pipeline continued to completion.
suggestion: Add a stage-level gating rule that blocks progression to ship when verify stage reports typecheck errors, unless the errors are explicitly whitelisted as pre-existing (not introduced by the current task changes).

pipelineFlaw.issue: Verify stage reported typecheck errors but pipeline proceeded to ship anyway
pipelineFlaw.evidence: verify stage completed with 0 retries despite errors in .next/types/validator.ts (PagesPageConfig type constraint failures). Ship stage then completed successfully and PR #573 was created.
